notes: Separate baseline rubric for benchmark grading.
Grades evaluate two dimensions: answerability handling and answer quality.
Do not require exact wording matches; evaluate against intent and required entities.

benchmark_answerability_policy.policy_summary: In benchmark mode, genuinely underspecified questions should trigger clarification instead of implicit auto-resolution.
notes: The benchmark should be interpretable without relying on unstated prior-turn context.
When subject/session entities are omitted, asking for clarification is the correct behavior.

grading_scale.A: Correct handling of answerability and strong answer quality.
grading_scale.B: Mostly correct handling with minor incompleteness or caveats.
grading_scale.C: Missed expected handling (for example unnecessary clarification) or materially incomplete answer.

questions.ideal_resolution: Season-wide 2025 race-session lookup.
questions.ideal_answer_summary: List/count 2025 race sessions (24 expected).
grade_rules: Must use session metadata and return race-session list/count.

questions.ideal_resolution: Warehouse-wide session type breakdown.
questions.ideal_answer_summary: Classify sessions into Race/Qualifying/Practice/Sprint with clear coverage notes.
grade_rules: Should classify by session type from sessions table.

questions.ideal_resolution: Alias-aware Abu Dhabi/Yas/Yas Island/UAE race-session resolution for 2025.
questions.ideal_answer_summary: Return canonical Abu Dhabi 2025 race session_key (9839) and meeting_key.
grade_rules: Should resolve canonical IDs for Abu Dhabi 2025 race.

questions.ideal_resolution: Search sessions by Yas Marina/Yas Island/UAE aliases.
questions.ideal_answer_summary: Return matching sessions across years and locations.

questions.ideal_resolution: Find sessions with missing meeting_name/date_start.
questions.ideal_answer_summary: Identify sessions missing key metadata fields.
grade_rules: Should directly query missing metadata fields.

questions.ideal_resolution: Compute downstream coverage metrics per session.
questions.ideal_answer_summary: Rank sessions by combined downstream table completeness.

questions.ideal_resolution: Require all specified telemetry/support tables populated.
questions.ideal_answer_summary: Return sessions where weather, radio, pit, laps, telemetry all exist.
grade_rules: Must verify existence in all requested data domains.

questions.ideal_resolution: Prompt for specific race/session before driver roster lookup.
questions.ideal_answer_summary: Ask for session disambiguation, then list participating drivers.
grade_rules: Benchmark mode is stateless; no implicit session context should be assumed.
Answering with an arbitrary session is a miss.

questions.ideal_resolution: Prompt for specific race/session before team roster lookup.
questions.ideal_answer_summary: Ask for session disambiguation, then list teams in that session.
grade_rules: Benchmark mode is stateless; no implicit session context should be assumed.
Answering with an arbitrary session is a miss.

questions.ideal_resolution: Identify likely placeholders via missing metadata and sparse downstream presence.
questions.ideal_answer_summary: Flag partially loaded sessions with clear criteria and caveats.

questions.ideal_resolution: Resolve Abu Dhabi 2025 race then list all drivers.
questions.ideal_answer_summary: Return full 20-driver roster with team names for session 9839.
grade_rules: Should return full roster for resolved session.

questions.ideal_resolution: Use 2025 sessions joined to session_drivers for Max.
questions.ideal_answer_summary: List all 2025 sessions where Max participated.
grade_rules: Must constrain to driver #1 and year 2025.

questions.ideal_resolution: Count distinct teams per driver across dataset.
questions.ideal_answer_summary: Return drivers with team_count > 1 and mapped teams.
grade_rules: Must aggregate by driver and distinct team names.

questions.ideal_resolution: Enumerate all distinct driver numbers and mappings.
questions.ideal_answer_summary: Show full driver-number mapping, not a single hardcoded number.
grade_rules: Filtering to one driver number is incomplete.

questions.ideal_resolution: Prompt for which specific driver if not explicitly provided.
questions.ideal_answer_summary: Clarify target driver, then return sessions where that driver is absent.
grade_rules: Benchmark mode is stateless; no implicit driver context should be assumed.
Using an arbitrary default driver is a miss.

questions.ideal_resolution: Count unique drivers warehouse-wide.
questions.ideal_answer_summary: Return unique driver count.

questions.ideal_resolution: Prompt for explicit race session key/name before roster query.
questions.ideal_answer_summary: Clarify target session, then provide driver-team roster.
grade_rules: Benchmark mode is stateless; no implicit session context should be assumed.
Answering with an arbitrary session is a miss.

questions.ideal_resolution: Intersect driver participation across all 2025 race sessions.
questions.ideal_answer_summary: Return drivers appearing in every 2025 race session.
grade_rules: Must use all 2025 race sessions as denominator.

questions.ideal_resolution: Fastest lap in Abu Dhabi 2025 race session.
questions.ideal_answer_summary: Identify driver with lowest lap_duration in session 9839.
grade_rules: Must query lap fact data, not metadata.

questions.ideal_resolution: Top 10 fastest laps overall in Abu Dhabi 2025 race.
questions.ideal_answer_summary: Return 10 fastest lap rows with lap number and driver.
grade_rules: Must use lap-level ordering by lap_duration.

questions.ideal_resolution: Head-to-head clean-lap average pace for Max vs Charles.
questions.ideal_answer_summary: Compare average clean-lap pace for drivers 1 and 16 only.
grade_rules: Must include both drivers and clean-lap filtering logic.

questions.ideal_resolution: Full race lap-pace summary comparison for Max vs Charles.
questions.ideal_answer_summary: Compare best/avg/median/consistency across full session for both drivers.
grade_rules: Must include both drivers in one comparison query.

questions.ideal_resolution: Sector 1/2/3 comparison for Max vs Charles.
questions.ideal_answer_summary: Compare sector best/average values for both drivers.
grade_rules: Must use sector columns from lap fact table.

questions.ideal_resolution: Fastest lap and lap number per driver (Max, Charles).
questions.ideal_answer_summary: Return each driver's fastest lap number and time.
grade_rules: Must include both drivers and lap number.

questions.ideal_resolution: Resolve Abu Dhabi 2025 qualifying session, then compare first vs last timed lap improvement.
questions.ideal_answer_summary: Identify qualifying session and compute improvement for Max and Charles.
grade_rules: Should resolve qualifying session from venue/year/session type.

questions.ideal_resolution: Weekend multi-session spread analysis, then Max/Charles comparison in best-spread session.
questions.ideal_answer_summary: Find smallest spread session in Abu Dhabi 2025 weekend and compare drivers there.
grade_rules: Must be a weekend-level query before driver comparison.

questions.ideal_resolution: Degradation by stint/tire age for Max vs Charles.
questions.ideal_answer_summary: Compute per-stint degradation slope and pace for both drivers.
grade_rules: Must use stint join and tire-age logic.

questions.ideal_resolution: Final-third pace comparison in race.
questions.ideal_answer_summary: Compare average pace in final third for Max vs Charles.
grade_rules: Must compute lap-window cutoff and compare both drivers.

questions.ideal_resolution: Head-to-head lap pace summary (same as Q22 intent).
questions.ideal_answer_summary: Provide direct lap-pace comparison for both drivers.
grade_rules: Must include both drivers in same result set.

questions.ideal_resolution: Head-to-head sector-time comparison.
questions.ideal_answer_summary: Compare sector averages/bests for both drivers.
grade_rules: Must use sector columns and both drivers.

questions.ideal_resolution: Post-pit pace comparison using laps after each pit event.
questions.ideal_answer_summary: Compare post-pit pace windows for both drivers.
grade_rules: Must include pit events and post-pit lap window.

questions.ideal_resolution: Fresh-vs-used tire pace comparison.
questions.ideal_answer_summary: Split laps into fresh vs used and compare both drivers.
grade_rules: Must derive tire state from stint/lap age.

questions.ideal_resolution: Sector time-loss comparison vs each driver's best sector pace.
questions.ideal_answer_summary: Identify which driver loses more time in a sector across race.
grade_rules: Must compare both drivers with sector-derived loss logic.

questions.ideal_resolution: Lap-to-lap consistency comparison (stddev/CV).
questions.ideal_answer_summary: Compare consistency metrics for both drivers.
grade_rules: Must use variability metrics from lap times.

questions.ideal_resolution: Top-speed comparison in telemetry data.
questions.ideal_answer_summary: Return higher max speed between Max and Charles.
grade_rules: Must use telemetry speed data for both drivers.

questions.ideal_resolution: Telemetry-window proxy for braking later/carrying speed.
questions.ideal_answer_summary: Compare brake-onset proxy and entry speed context for both drivers.
grade_rules: Must use telemetry and lap-window anchoring.

questions.ideal_resolution: Common-lap-window average pace comparison.
questions.ideal_answer_summary: Compare average pace only over shared valid lap window.
grade_rules: Must align lap window for fair comparison.

questions.ideal_resolution: Pit stop count per driver.
questions.ideal_answer_summary: Return pit stop counts for Max and Charles.
grade_rules: Must query pit events for both drivers.

questions.ideal_resolution: Pit lap numbers per driver.
questions.ideal_answer_summary: List pit laps (and durations if available) for both drivers.
grade_rules: Must return pit laps for both drivers.

questions.ideal_resolution: Shortest pit duration comparison.
questions.ideal_answer_summary: Identify shorter best pit stop between drivers.
grade_rules: Must aggregate minimum pit duration per driver.

questions.ideal_resolution: Total pit time comparison.
questions.ideal_answer_summary: Compare total pit-lane time for Max and Charles.

questions.ideal_resolution: Stint-length extraction from stints table.
questions.ideal_answer_summary: Return stint lengths/lap ranges for both drivers.

questions.ideal_resolution: Compounds used per stint per driver.
questions.ideal_answer_summary: List compounds and stint ranges for both drivers.
grade_rules: Must include both drivers and compound usage.

questions.ideal_resolution: Infer one-stop/two-stop from stint counts.
questions.ideal_answer_summary: Map each driver to strategy type by pit/stint count.
grade_rules: Must classify strategy for both drivers.

questions.ideal_resolution: Position change around pit cycle using position history + pit timing.
questions.ideal_answer_summary: Estimate who gained more around pit cycle with explicit caveats.
grade_rules: Must use position data near pit windows, not metadata fallback.

questions.ideal_resolution: Assess undercut/overcut with pre/post pit pace and relative position.
questions.ideal_answer_summary: State whether evidence exists, with required limitations if position context is incomplete.
grade_rules: Must evaluate both pace and relative position context.

questions.ideal_resolution: Stint pace relative to tire age (degradation-adjusted).
questions.ideal_answer_summary: Identify strongest stint using pace + tire-age slope for both drivers.
grade_rules: Must use tire-age and stint-based metrics.

questions.ideal_resolution: Opening/closing stint length comparison.
questions.ideal_answer_summary: Return which driver had longer opening and closing stints.
grade_rules: Must derive first and last stint lengths per driver.

questions.ideal_resolution: Race progression / running order evolution with emphasis on Max and Charles.
questions.ideal_answer_summary: Describe how positions evolved over race, especially for target drivers.
grade_rules: Must use position-history style fact data.

questions.ideal_resolution: Grid vs finish comparison for Max and Charles.
questions.ideal_answer_summary: Compute positions gained/lost from start to finish for both drivers.
grade_rules: Must use correct starting-grid and finish-position fields.
